pi_items.source: Task description explicit instructions and export_result.sh which searches for this exact file path.
A 3x1 montage of three 1600x1600 FITS images (scaled by 0.5 to 2400x800 pixels) containing contrast-stretched real nebular data as PNG will inherently exceed 20 KB in size.
Calculated logically from the task instructions: placing three identical images (1600x1600 pixels) in a 3 columns by 1 row configuration natively yields a 3:1 width-to-height aspect ratio.
Serves as a valid acceptable error margin for scoring if the agent adds unrequested padding or borders, complementing the exact 3.0 mathematical expectation.
Confirmed via environment installation scripts (install_astroimagej.sh) which download real astronomical data directly from https://esahubble.org/static/projects/fits_liberator/datasets/eagle/.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real astronomical FITS data of the Eagle Nebula, downloaded from the ESA Hubble FITS Liberator dataset. The task requires a 3x1 image montage scaled by 0.5. Given the original 1600x1600 dimensions of the FITS files, the resulting image will be 2400x800 pixels, mathematically confirming the expected 3.0 aspect ratio. The minimum size threshold of 20 KB is appropriate for a high-contrast 2400x800 PNG, and the expected output path is explicitly defined by the task instructions and export script. Notably, the verifier.py script is entirely missing from the environment.
